turns.student: What brings you in today?
turns.patient: I've been having some problems with my stomach lately. I've had crampy pain on the left side, and I'm going to the bathroom a lot more than usual - like 8 or 10 times a day, and it's all loose and watery.

turns.student: Can you describe the main symptom you're experiencing?
turns.patient: The crampy pain is definitely the worst part. It's like a sharp stabbing feeling on my left side, and it comes and goes, but when it's there, it really hurts - I'd say 7 or 8 out of 10 in terms of how bad it feels. The diarrhea is also super uncomfortable, especially because it's so frequent and urgent.

turns.student: When did this start?
turns.patient: It started about a week ago, but to be honest, I've been feeling pretty crummy for even longer than that - just not as severely. But over the past 7-8 days, it's gotten so bad that I can barely keep up with work and I'm starting to feel really run down.

turns.student: How often does it happen?
turns.patient: It happens about every hour, sometimes more often. I'd say on average, I'm going to the bathroom around 8-10 times a day, with some days being worse than others.

turns.student: How severe is it on a scale of 1 to 10?
turns.patient: Honestly, right now it would be a solid 9 out of 10. The pain and discomfort are really getting in the way of my daily life, and I'm just feeling so drained from not being able to eat or drink much without having an episode.

turns.student: Do you notice any other symptoms when it happens?
turns.patient: Yeah, I've been feeling pretty fatigued lately, especially after each episode. It's like my body is just exhausted from trying to deal with everything else that's going on down there. And sometimes, I get a little lightheaded or dizzy when I'm getting ready to go to the bathroom because I know it's gonna be one of those super urgent ones.

turns.student: Does it get worse with any particular activity?
turns.patient: It definitely gets worse with stress, and since my job is pretty high-stress anyway, that doesn't help. When I've been working on tight deadlines or dealing with a lot of pressure at work, the symptoms seem to flare up even more.

turns.student: Do you have any sweating, nausea, or dizziness with it?
turns.patient: Only the dizziness, really. It's usually just when I'm getting ready to go to the bathroom and I know it's gonna be one of those super urgent ones. Sometimes I feel a bit lightheaded after an episode too, but nothing too severe.

turns.student: Does the pain or discomfort spread to any other part of your body?
turns.patient: The pain mainly stays on my left side, but sometimes it can radiate up towards my lower back. It's not a burning sensation like you'd get with acid reflux, more just a sharp stabbing feeling that feels like it's trying to move upwards, but mostly it's still centered around my belly button area.

turns.student: Do you have any swelling in your legs or feet?
turns.patient: I don't think so. I mean, I've been a bit more tired than usual and my energy levels are really low, but I haven't noticed any swelling in my legs or feet. To be honest, I'm more worried about what's going on with my stomach right now. It's just been such a disruption to my daily life, and it's starting to affect my work and overall mood.

turns.student: What medications are you currently taking?
turns.patient: I've actually been self-medicating with loperamide (Imodium) for diarrhea, but I have to say it's only provided minimal relief. It helps a bit with the frequency of bowel movements, but the pain and discomfort haven't really gone away.

turns.student: Do you have any medical conditions like high blood pressure or diabetes?
turns.patient: My dad has type 2 diabetes, but I don't know if that means I'm more likely to get something similar. My mom is healthy, though.

turns.student: Have you ever had your cholesterol checked?
turns.patient: I'm not sure about my cholesterol levels - I haven't had any bloodwork done in a while, actually. I think the last time was when I got my physical for work insurance, but that was over two years ago. To be honest, I hadn't really thought about it until now with all these other symptoms going on.

turns.student: Do you smoke or drink alcohol?
turns.patient: I'm a non-smoker, and to be honest, I don't really drink much alcohol either. I mean, I'll have a beer every now and then with friends, but I wouldn't say it's a regular thing for me. To be frank, with my current symptoms, I've been trying to cut back on everything that might make things worse, including coffee and spicy food, just because it seems like anything can trigger an episode right now.

turns.student: How would you describe your diet and exercise habits?
turns.patient: To be honest, my diet has been pretty irregular lately due to long work hours. I've been grabbing takeout a lot more often than I should, which isn't helping with my stomach issues. As for exercise, I try to get in some sort of physical activity during my lunch break or after work, but it's not as consistent as I'd like it to be.

turns.student: Has anyone in your family had heart disease or heart attacks?
turns.patient: To be honest, I'm not sure about heart disease specifically. My dad has type 2 diabetes, and my mom is healthy, but I don't know if there's a history of heart problems on either side of my family. But to change gears a bit, my older sister was diagnosed with Crohn's disease at age 30, which might be relevant in some way?

turns.student: Has anyone in your family had other serious medical conditions?
turns.patient: My older sister was diagnosed with Crohn's disease at age 30. That's about it that comes to mind right now. I'm not aware of any other serious medical conditions on either side of my family.

turns.student: When was the last time you saw a doctor?
turns.patient: I haven't seen a doctor in over 2 years, no recent bloodwork or tests were done during that time. I've been trying to take care of myself and didn't think much of my stomach issues until they started getting this bad.

turns.student: Do you have any allergies to medications?
turns.patient: No, I don't know of any medication allergies. To be honest, I've only been taking loperamide (Imodium) for a couple of weeks now, and it hasn't seemed to help much with my symptoms, so I haven't really thought about other medications that might interact with it or cause problems.

turns.student: Is there anything else you think I should know about your health?
turns.patient: Actually, yes. I've been experiencing some unintentional weight loss over the past few weeks - about 4 kg (9 lbs) in total. It's definitely affected my mood and energy levels, which isn't helping with my overall fatigue from the diarrhea. And to be honest, it's getting pretty frustrating because I feel like I'm just barely holding on at this point, between work and dealing with these symptoms nonstop.
